4886	if (IS_OUTER_JOIN(best_path->jpath.jointype))
4887	{
4888		extract_actual_join_clauses(joinclauses,
4889									best_path->jpath.path.parent->relids,
4890									&joinclauses, &otherclauses);
4891	}
4892	else
4893	{
4894		/* We can treat all clauses alike for an inner join */
4895		joinclauses = extract_actual_clauses(joinclauses, false);
4896		otherclauses = NIL;
4897	}
4898
4899	/*
4900	 * Remove the hashclauses from the list of join qual clauses, leaving the
4901	 * list of quals that must be checked as qpquals.
4902	 */
4903	hashclauses = get_actual_clauses(best_path->path_hashclauses);
4904	joinclauses = list_difference(joinclauses, hashclauses);
4905
4906	/*
4907	 * Replace any outer-relation variables with nestloop params.  There
4908	 * should not be any in the hashclauses.
4909	 */
4910	if (best_path->jpath.path.param_info)
4911	{
4912		joinclauses = (List *)
4913			replace_nestloop_params(root, (Node *) joinclauses);
4914		otherclauses = (List *)
4915			replace_nestloop_params(root, (Node *) otherclauses);
4916	}
4917
4918	/*
4919	 * Rearrange hashclauses, if needed, so that the outer variable is always
4920	 * on the left.
4921	 */
4922	hashclauses = get_switched_clauses(best_path->path_hashclauses,
4923									   best_path->jpath.outerjoinpath->parent->relids);
4924
4925	/*
4926	 * If there is a single join clause and we can identify the outer variable
4927	 * as a simple column reference, supply its identity for possible use in
4928	 * skew optimization.  (Note: in principle we could do skew optimization
4929	 * with multiple join clauses, but we'd have to be able to determine the
4930	 * most common combinations of outer values, which we don't currently have
4931	 * enough stats for.)
4932	 */
4933	if (list_length(hashclauses) == 1)
4934	{
4935		OpExpr	   *clause = (OpExpr *) linitial(hashclauses);
4936		Node	   *node;
4937
4938		Assert(is_opclause(clause));
4939		node = (Node *) linitial(clause->args);
4940		if (IsA(node, RelabelType))
4941			node = (Node *) ((RelabelType *) node)->arg;
4942		if (IsA(node, Var))
4943		{
4944			Var		   *var = (Var *) node;
4945			RangeTblEntry *rte;
4946
4947			rte = root->simple_rte_array[var->varno];
4948			if (rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION)
4949			{
4950				skewTable = rte->relid;
4951				skewColumn = var->varattno;
4952				skewInherit = rte->inh;
4953			}
4954		}
4955	}
4956
4957	/*
4958	 * Collect hash related information. The hashed expressions are
4959	 * deconstructed into outer/inner expressions, so they can be computed
4960	 * separately (inner expressions are used to build the hashtable via Hash,
4961	 * outer expressions to perform lookups of tuples from HashJoin's outer
4962	 * plan in the hashtable). Also collect operator information necessary to
4963	 * build the hashtable.
4964	 */
4965	foreach(lc, hashclauses)
4966	{
4967		OpExpr	   *hclause = lfirst_node(OpExpr, lc);
4968
4969		hashoperators = lappend_oid(hashoperators, hclause->opno);
4970		hashcollations = lappend_oid(hashcollations, hclause->inputcollid);
4971		outer_hashkeys = lappend(outer_hashkeys, linitial(hclause->args));
4972		inner_hashkeys = lappend(inner_hashkeys, lsecond(hclause->args));
4973	}
4974
4975	/*
4976	 * Build the hash node and hash join node.
4977	 */
4978	hash_plan = make_hash(inner_plan,
4979						  inner_hashkeys,
4980						  skewTable,
4981						  skewColumn,
4982						  skewInherit);
4983
4984	/*
4985	 * Set Hash node's startup & total costs equal to total cost of input
4986	 * plan; this only affects EXPLAIN display not decisions.
4987	 */
4988	copy_plan_costsize(&hash_plan->plan, inner_plan);
4989	hash_plan->plan.startup_cost = hash_plan->plan.total_cost;
4990
4991	/*
4992	 * If parallel-aware, the executor will also need an estimate of the total
4993	 * number of rows expected from all participants so that it can size the
4994	 * shared hash table.
4995	 */
4996	if (best_path->jpath.path.parallel_aware)
4997	{
4998		hash_plan->plan.parallel_aware = true;
4999		hash_plan->rows_total = best_path->inner_rows_total;
5000	}
5001
5002	join_plan = make_hashjoin(tlist,
5003							  joinclauses,
5004							  otherclauses,
5005							  hashclauses,
5006							  hashoperators,
5007							  hashcollations,
5008							  outer_hashkeys,
5009							  outer_plan,
5010							  (Plan *) hash_plan,
5011							  best_path->jpath.jointype,
5012							  best_path->jpath.inner_unique);
5013
5014	copy_generic_path_info(&join_plan->join.plan, &best_path->jpath.path);
5015
5016	return join_plan;
5017}
5018
5019
5020/*****************************************************************************
5021 *
5022 *	SUPPORTING ROUTINES
5023 *
5024 *****************************************************************************/
5025
5026/*
5027 * replace_nestloop_params
5028 *	  Replace outer-relation Vars and PlaceHolderVars in the given expression
5029 *	  with nestloop Params
5030 *
5031 * All Vars and PlaceHolderVars belonging to the relation(s) identified by
5032 * root->curOuterRels are replaced by Params, and entries are added to
5033 * root->curOuterParams if not already present.
5034 */
5035static Node *
5036replace_nestloop_params(PlannerInfo *root, Node *expr)
5037{
5038	/* No setup needed for tree walk, so away we go */
5039	return replace_nestloop_params_mutator(expr, root);
5040}
5041
5042static Node *
5043replace_nestloop_params_mutator(Node *node, PlannerInfo *root)
5044{
5045	if (node == NULL)
5046		return NULL;
5047	if (IsA(node, Var))
5048	{
5049		Var		   *var = (Var *) node;
5050
5051		/* Upper-level Vars should be long gone at this point */
5052		Assert(var->varlevelsup == 0);
5053		/* If not to be replaced, we can just return the Var unmodified */
5054		if (IS_SPECIAL_VARNO(var->varno) ||
5055			!bms_is_member(var->varno, root->curOuterRels))
5056			return node;
5057		/* Replace the Var with a nestloop Param */
5058		return (Node *) replace_nestloop_param_var(root, var);
5059	}
5060	if (IsA(node, PlaceHolderVar))
5061	{
5062		PlaceHolderVar *phv = (PlaceHolderVar *) node;
5063
5064		/* Upper-level PlaceHolderVars should be long gone at this point */
5065		Assert(phv->phlevelsup == 0);
5066
5067		/* Check whether we need to replace the PHV */
5068		if (!bms_is_subset(find_placeholder_info(root, phv)->ph_eval_at,
5069						   root->curOuterRels))
5070		{
5071			/*
5072			 * We can't replace the whole PHV, but we might still need to
5073			 * replace Vars or PHVs within its expression, in case it ends up
5074			 * actually getting evaluated here.  (It might get evaluated in
5075			 * this plan node, or some child node; in the latter case we don't
5076			 * really need to process the expression here, but we haven't got
5077			 * enough info to tell if that's the case.)  Flat-copy the PHV
5078			 * node and then recurse on its expression.
5079			 *
5080			 * Note that after doing this, we might have different
5081			 * representations of the contents of the same PHV in different
5082			 * parts of the plan tree.  This is OK because equal() will just
5083			 * match on phid/phlevelsup, so setrefs.c will still recognize an
5084			 * upper-level reference to a lower-level copy of the same PHV.
5085			 */
5086			PlaceHolderVar *newphv = makeNode(PlaceHolderVar);
5087
5088			memcpy(newphv, phv, sizeof(PlaceHolderVar));
5089			newphv->phexpr = (Expr *)
5090				replace_nestloop_params_mutator((Node *) phv->phexpr,
5091												root);
5092			return (Node *) newphv;
5093		}
5094		/* Replace the PlaceHolderVar with a nestloop Param */
5095		return (Node *) replace_nestloop_param_placeholdervar(root, phv);
5096	}
5097	return expression_tree_mutator(node, replace_nestloop_params_mutator, root);
5098}
5099
5100/*
5101 * fix_indexqual_references
5102 *	  Adjust indexqual clauses to the form the executor's indexqual
5103 *	  machinery needs.
5104 *
5105 * We have three tasks here:
5106 *	* Select the actual qual clauses out of the input IndexClause list,
5107 *	  and remove RestrictInfo nodes from the qual clauses.
5108 *	* Replace any outer-relation Var or PHV nodes with nestloop Params.
5109 *	  (XXX eventually, that responsibility should go elsewhere?)
5110 *	* Index keys must be represented by Var nodes with varattno set to the
5111 *	  index's attribute number, not the attribute number in the original rel.
5112 *
5113 * *stripped_indexquals_p receives a list of the actual qual clauses.
5114 *
5115 * *fixed_indexquals_p receives a list of the adjusted quals.  This is a copy
5116 * that shares no substructure with the original; this is needed in case there
5117 * are subplans in it (we need two separate copies of the subplan tree, or
5118 * things will go awry).
5119 */
5120static void
5121fix_indexqual_references(PlannerInfo *root, IndexPath *index_path,
5122						 List **stripped_indexquals_p, List **fixed_indexquals_p)
5123{
5124	IndexOptInfo *index = index_path->indexinfo;
5125	List	   *stripped_indexquals;
5126	List	   *fixed_indexquals;
5127	ListCell   *lc;
5128
5129	stripped_indexquals = fixed_indexquals = NIL;
5130
5131	foreach(lc, index_path->indexclauses)
5132	{
5133		IndexClause *iclause = lfirst_node(IndexClause, lc);
5134		int			indexcol = iclause->indexcol;
5135		ListCell   *lc2;
5136
5137		foreach(lc2, iclause->indexquals)
5138		{
5139			RestrictInfo *rinfo = lfirst_node(RestrictInfo, lc2);
5140			Node	   *clause = (Node *) rinfo->clause;
5141
5142			stripped_indexquals = lappend(stripped_indexquals, clause);
5143			clause = fix_indexqual_clause(root, index, indexcol,
5144										  clause, iclause->indexcols);
5145			fixed_indexquals = lappend(fixed_indexquals, clause);
5146		}
5147	}
5148
5149	*stripped_indexquals_p = stripped_indexquals;
5150	*fixed_indexquals_p = fixed_indexquals;
5151}
5152
5153/*
5154 * fix_indexorderby_references
5155 *	  Adjust indexorderby clauses to the form the executor's index
5156 *	  machinery needs.
5157 *
5158 * This is a simplified version of fix_indexqual_references.  The input is
5159 * bare clauses and a separate indexcol list, instead of IndexClauses.
5160 */
5161static List *
5162fix_indexorderby_references(PlannerInfo *root, IndexPath *index_path)
5163{
5164	IndexOptInfo *index = index_path->indexinfo;
5165	List	   *fixed_indexorderbys;
5166	ListCell   *lcc,
5167			   *lci;
5168
5169	fixed_indexorderbys = NIL;
5170
5171	forboth(lcc, index_path->indexorderbys, lci, index_path->indexorderbycols)
5172	{
5173		Node	   *clause = (Node *) lfirst(lcc);
5174		int			indexcol = lfirst_int(lci);
5175
5176		clause = fix_indexqual_clause(root, index, indexcol, clause, NIL);
5177		fixed_indexorderbys = lappend(fixed_indexorderbys, clause);
5178	}
5179
5180	return fixed_indexorderbys;
5181}
5182
5183/*
5184 * fix_indexqual_clause
5185 *	  Convert a single indexqual clause to the form needed by the executor.
5186 *
5187 * We replace nestloop params here, and replace the index key variables
5188 * or expressions by index Var nodes.
5189 */
5190static Node *
5191fix_indexqual_clause(PlannerInfo *root, IndexOptInfo *index, int indexcol,
5192					 Node *clause, List *indexcolnos)
5193{
5194	/*
5195	 * Replace any outer-relation variables with nestloop params.
5196	 *
5197	 * This also makes a copy of the clause, so it's safe to modify it
5198	 * in-place below.
5199	 */
5200	clause = replace_nestloop_params(root, clause);
5201
5202	if (IsA(clause, OpExpr))
5203	{
5204		OpExpr	   *op = (OpExpr *) clause;
5205
5206		/* Replace the indexkey expression with an index Var. */
5207		linitial(op->args) = fix_indexqual_operand(linitial(op->args),
5208												   index,
5209												   indexcol);
5210	}
5211	else if (IsA(clause, RowCompareExpr))
5212	{
5213		RowCompareExpr *rc = (RowCompareExpr *) clause;
5214		ListCell   *lca,
5215				   *lcai;
5216
5217		/* Replace the indexkey expressions with index Vars. */
5218		Assert(list_length(rc->largs) == list_length(indexcolnos));
5219		forboth(lca, rc->largs, lcai, indexcolnos)
5220		{
5221			lfirst(lca) = fix_indexqual_operand(lfirst(lca),
5222												index,
5223												lfirst_int(lcai));
5224		}
5225	}
5226	else if (IsA(clause, ScalarArrayOpExpr))
5227	{
5228		ScalarArrayOpExpr *saop = (ScalarArrayOpExpr *) clause;
5229
5230		/* Replace the indexkey expression with an index Var. */
5231		linitial(saop->args) = fix_indexqual_operand(linitial(saop->args),
5232													 index,
5233													 indexcol);
5234	}
5235	else if (IsA(clause, NullTest))
5236	{
5237		NullTest   *nt = (NullTest *) clause;
5238
5239		/* Replace the indexkey expression with an index Var. */
5240		nt->arg = (Expr *) fix_indexqual_operand((Node *) nt->arg,
5241												 index,
5242												 indexcol);
5243	}
5244	else
5245		elog(ERROR, "unsupported indexqual type: %d",
5246			 (int) nodeTag(clause));
5247
5248	return clause;
5249}
5250
5251/*
5252 * fix_indexqual_operand
5253 *	  Convert an indexqual expression to a Var referencing the index column.
5254 *
5255 * We represent index keys by Var nodes having varno == INDEX_VAR and varattno
5256 * equal to the index's attribute number (index column position).
5257 *
5258 * Most of the code here is just for sanity cross-checking that the given
5259 * expression actually matches the index column it's claimed to.  It should
5260 * match the logic in match_index_to_operand().
5261 */
5262static Node *
5263fix_indexqual_operand(Node *node, IndexOptInfo *index, int indexcol)
5264{
5265	Var		   *result;
5266	int			pos;
5267	ListCell   *indexpr_item;
5268
5269	Assert(indexcol >= 0 && indexcol < index->ncolumns);
5270
5271	/*
5272	 * Remove any PlaceHolderVar wrapping of the indexkey
5273	 */
5274	node = strip_noop_phvs(node);
5275
5276	/*
5277	 * Remove any binary-compatible relabeling of the indexkey
5278	 */
5279	while (IsA(node, RelabelType))
5280		node = (Node *) ((RelabelType *) node)->arg;
5281
5282	if (index->indexkeys[indexcol] != 0)
5283	{
5284		/* It's a simple index column */
5285		if (IsA(node, Var) &&
5286			((Var *) node)->varno == index->rel->relid &&
5287			((Var *) node)->varattno == index->indexkeys[indexcol])
5288		{
5289			result = (Var *) copyObject(node);
5290			result->varno = INDEX_VAR;
5291			result->varattno = indexcol + 1;
5292			return (Node *) result;
5293		}
5294		else
5295			elog(ERROR, "index key does not match expected index column");
5296	}
5297
5298	/* It's an index expression, so find and cross-check the expression */
5299	indexpr_item = list_head(index->indexprs);
5300	for (pos = 0; pos < index->ncolumns; pos++)
5301	{
5302		if (index->indexkeys[pos] == 0)
5303		{
5304			if (indexpr_item == NULL)
5305				elog(ERROR, "too few entries in indexprs list");
5306			if (pos == indexcol)
5307			{
5308				Node	   *indexkey;
5309
5310				indexkey = (Node *) lfirst(indexpr_item);
5311				if (indexkey && IsA(indexkey, RelabelType))
5312					indexkey = (Node *) ((RelabelType *) indexkey)->arg;
5313				if (equal(node, indexkey))
5314				{
5315					result = makeVar(INDEX_VAR, indexcol + 1,
5316									 exprType(lfirst(indexpr_item)), -1,
5317									 exprCollation(lfirst(indexpr_item)),
5318									 0);
5319					return (Node *) result;
5320				}
5321				else
5322					elog(ERROR, "index key does not match expected index column");
5323			}
5324			indexpr_item = lnext(index->indexprs, indexpr_item);
5325		}
5326	}
5327
5328	/* Oops... */
5329	elog(ERROR, "index key does not match expected index column");
5330	return NULL;				/* keep compiler quiet */
5331}
5332
5333/*
5334 * get_switched_clauses
5335 *	  Given a list of merge or hash joinclauses (as RestrictInfo nodes),
5336 *	  extract the bare clauses, and rearrange the elements within the
5337 *	  clauses, if needed, so the outer join variable is on the left and
5338 *	  the inner is on the right.  The original clause data structure is not
5339 *	  touched; a modified list is returned.  We do, however, set the transient
5340 *	  outer_is_left field in each RestrictInfo to show which side was which.
5341 */
5342static List *
5343get_switched_clauses(List *clauses, Relids outerrelids)
5344{
5345	List	   *t_list = NIL;
5346	ListCell   *l;
5347
5348	foreach(l, clauses)
5349	{
5350		RestrictInfo *restrictinfo = (RestrictInfo *) lfirst(l);
5351		OpExpr	   *clause = (OpExpr *) restrictinfo->clause;
5352
5353		Assert(is_opclause(clause));
5354		if (bms_is_subset(restrictinfo->right_relids, outerrelids))
5355		{
5356			/*
5357			 * Duplicate just enough of the structure to allow commuting the
5358			 * clause without changing the original list.  Could use
5359			 * copyObject, but a complete deep copy is overkill.
5360			 */
5361			OpExpr	   *temp = makeNode(OpExpr);
5362
5363			temp->opno = clause->opno;
5364			temp->opfuncid = InvalidOid;
5365			temp->opresulttype = clause->opresulttype;
5366			temp->opretset = clause->opretset;
5367			temp->opcollid = clause->opcollid;
5368			temp->inputcollid = clause->inputcollid;
5369			temp->args = list_copy(clause->args);
5370			temp->location = clause->location;
5371			/* Commute it --- note this modifies the temp node in-place. */
5372			CommuteOpExpr(temp);
5373			t_list = lappend(t_list, temp);
5374			restrictinfo->outer_is_left = false;
5375		}
5376		else
5377		{
5378			Assert(bms_is_subset(restrictinfo->left_relids, outerrelids));
5379			t_list = lappend(t_list, clause);
5380			restrictinfo->outer_is_left = true;
5381		}
5382	}
5383	return t_list;
5384}
5385
5386/*
5387 * order_qual_clauses
5388 *		Given a list of qual clauses that will all be evaluated at the same
5389 *		plan node, sort the list into the order we want to check the quals
5390 *		in at runtime.
5391 *
5392 * When security barrier quals are used in the query, we may have quals with
5393 * different security levels in the list.  Quals of lower security_level
5394 * must go before quals of higher security_level, except that we can grant
5395 * exceptions to move up quals that are leakproof.  When security level
5396 * doesn't force the decision, we prefer to order clauses by estimated
5397 * execution cost, cheapest first.
5398 *
5399 * Ideally the order should be driven by a combination of execution cost and
5400 * selectivity, but it's not immediately clear how to account for both,
5401 * and given the uncertainty of the estimates the reliability of the decisions
5402 * would be doubtful anyway.  So we just order by security level then
5403 * estimated per-tuple cost, being careful not to change the order when
5404 * (as is often the case) the estimates are identical.
5405 *
5406 * Although this will work on either bare clauses or RestrictInfos, it's
5407 * much faster to apply it to RestrictInfos, since it can re-use cost
5408 * information that is cached in RestrictInfos.  XXX in the bare-clause
5409 * case, we are also not able to apply security considerations.  That is
5410 * all right for the moment, because the bare-clause case doesn't occur
5411 * anywhere that barrier quals could be present, but it would be better to
5412 * get rid of it.
5413 *
5414 * Note: some callers pass lists that contain entries that will later be
5415 * removed; this is the easiest way to let this routine see RestrictInfos
5416 * instead of bare clauses.  This is another reason why trying to consider
5417 * selectivity in the ordering would likely do the wrong thing.
5418 */
5419static List *
5420order_qual_clauses(PlannerInfo *root, List *clauses)
5421{
5422	typedef struct
5423	{
5424		Node	   *clause;
5425		Cost		cost;
5426		Index		security_level;
5427	} QualItem;
5428	int			nitems = list_length(clauses);
5429	QualItem   *items;
5430	ListCell   *lc;
5431	int			i;
5432	List	   *result;
5433
5434	/* No need to work hard for 0 or 1 clause */
5435	if (nitems <= 1)
5436		return clauses;
5437
5438	/*
5439	 * Collect the items and costs into an array.  This is to avoid repeated
5440	 * cost_qual_eval work if the inputs aren't RestrictInfos.
5441	 */
5442	items = (QualItem *) palloc(nitems * sizeof(QualItem));
5443	i = 0;
5444	foreach(lc, clauses)
5445	{
5446		Node	   *clause = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
5447		QualCost	qcost;
5448
5449		cost_qual_eval_node(&qcost, clause, root);
5450		items[i].clause = clause;
5451		items[i].cost = qcost.per_tuple;
5452		if (IsA(clause, RestrictInfo))
5453		{
5454			RestrictInfo *rinfo = (RestrictInfo *) clause;
5455
5456			/*
5457			 * If a clause is leakproof, it doesn't have to be constrained by
5458			 * its nominal security level.  If it's also reasonably cheap
5459			 * (here defined as 10X cpu_operator_cost), pretend it has
5460			 * security_level 0, which will allow it to go in front of
5461			 * more-expensive quals of lower security levels.  Of course, that
5462			 * will also force it to go in front of cheaper quals of its own
5463			 * security level, which is not so great, but we can alleviate
5464			 * that risk by applying the cost limit cutoff.
5465			 */
5466			if (rinfo->leakproof && items[i].cost < 10 * cpu_operator_cost)
5467				items[i].security_level = 0;
5468			else
5469				items[i].security_level = rinfo->security_level;
5470		}
5471		else
5472			items[i].security_level = 0;
5473		i++;
5474	}
5475
5476	/*
5477	 * Sort.  We don't use qsort() because it's not guaranteed stable for
5478	 * equal keys.  The expected number of entries is small enough that a
5479	 * simple insertion sort should be good enough.
5480	 */
5481	for (i = 1; i < nitems; i++)
5482	{
5483		QualItem	newitem = items[i];
5484		int			j;
5485
5486		/* insert newitem into the already-sorted subarray */
5487		for (j = i; j > 0; j--)
5488		{
5489			QualItem   *olditem = &items[j - 1];
5490
5491			if (newitem.security_level > olditem->security_level ||
5492				(newitem.security_level == olditem->security_level &&
5493				 newitem.cost >= olditem->cost))
5494				break;
5495			items[j] = *olditem;
5496		}
5497		items[j] = newitem;
5498	}
5499
5500	/* Convert back to a list */
5501	result = NIL;
5502	for (i = 0; i < nitems; i++)
5503		result = lappend(result, items[i].clause);
5504
5505	return result;
5506}
5507
5508/*
5509 * Copy cost and size info from a Path node to the Plan node created from it.
5510 * The executor usually won't use this info, but it's needed by EXPLAIN.
5511 * Also copy the parallel-related flags, which the executor *will* use.
5512 */
5513static void
5514copy_generic_path_info(Plan *dest, Path *src)
5515{
5516	dest->disabled_nodes = src->disabled_nodes;
5517	dest->startup_cost = src->startup_cost;
5518	dest->total_cost = src->total_cost;
5519	dest->plan_rows = src->rows;
5520	dest->plan_width = src->pathtarget->width;
5521	dest->parallel_aware = src->parallel_aware;
5522	dest->parallel_safe = src->parallel_safe;
5523}
5524
5525/*
5526 * Copy cost and size info from a lower plan node to an inserted node.
5527 * (Most callers alter the info after copying it.)
5528 */
5529static void
5530copy_plan_costsize(Plan *dest, Plan *src)
5531{
5532	dest->disabled_nodes = src->disabled_nodes;
5533	dest->startup_cost = src->startup_cost;
5534	dest->total_cost = src->total_cost;
5535	dest->plan_rows = src->plan_rows;
5536	dest->plan_width = src->plan_width;
5537	/* Assume the inserted node is not parallel-aware. */
5538	dest->parallel_aware = false;
5539	/* Assume the inserted node is parallel-safe, if child plan is. */
5540	dest->parallel_safe = src->parallel_safe;
5541}
5542
5543/*
5544 * Some places in this file build Sort nodes that don't have a directly
5545 * corresponding Path node.  The cost of the sort is, or should have been,
5546 * included in the cost of the Path node we're working from, but since it's
5547 * not split out, we have to re-figure it using cost_sort().  This is just
5548 * to label the Sort node nicely for EXPLAIN.
5549 *
5550 * limit_tuples is as for cost_sort (in particular, pass -1 if no limit)
5551 */
5552static void
5553label_sort_with_costsize(PlannerInfo *root, Sort *plan, double limit_tuples)
5554{
5555	Plan	   *lefttree = plan->plan.lefttree;
5556	Path		sort_path;		/* dummy for result of cost_sort */
5557
5558	Assert(IsA(plan, Sort));
5559
5560	cost_sort(&sort_path, root, NIL,
5561			  plan->plan.disabled_nodes,
5562			  lefttree->total_cost,
5563			  lefttree->plan_rows,
5564			  lefttree->plan_width,
5565			  0.0,
5566			  work_mem,
5567			  limit_tuples);
5568	plan->plan.startup_cost = sort_path.startup_cost;
5569	plan->plan.total_cost = sort_path.total_cost;
5570	plan->plan.plan_rows = lefttree->plan_rows;
5571	plan->plan.plan_width = lefttree->plan_width;
5572	plan->plan.parallel_aware = false;
5573	plan->plan.parallel_safe = lefttree->parallel_safe;
5574}
5575
5576/*
5577 * Same as label_sort_with_costsize, but labels the IncrementalSort node
5578 * instead.
5579 */
5580static void
5581label_incrementalsort_with_costsize(PlannerInfo *root, IncrementalSort *plan,
5582									List *pathkeys, double limit_tuples)
5583{
5584	Plan	   *lefttree = plan->sort.plan.lefttree;
5585	Path		sort_path;		/* dummy for result of cost_incremental_sort */
5586
5587	Assert(IsA(plan, IncrementalSort));
5588
5589	cost_incremental_sort(&sort_path, root, pathkeys,
5590						  plan->nPresortedCols,
5591						  plan->sort.plan.disabled_nodes,
5592						  lefttree->startup_cost,
5593						  lefttree->total_cost,
5594						  lefttree->plan_rows,
5595						  lefttree->plan_width,
5596						  0.0,
5597						  work_mem,
5598						  limit_tuples);
5599	plan->sort.plan.startup_cost = sort_path.startup_cost;
5600	plan->sort.plan.total_cost = sort_path.total_cost;
5601	plan->sort.plan.plan_rows = lefttree->plan_rows;
5602	plan->sort.plan.plan_width = lefttree->plan_width;
5603	plan->sort.plan.parallel_aware = false;
5604	plan->sort.plan.parallel_safe = lefttree->parallel_safe;
5605}
5606
5607/*
5608 * bitmap_subplan_mark_shared
5609 *	 Set isshared flag in bitmap subplan so that it will be created in
5610 *	 shared memory.
5611 */
5612static void
5613bitmap_subplan_mark_shared(Plan *plan)
5614{
5615	if (IsA(plan, BitmapAnd))
5616		bitmap_subplan_mark_shared(linitial(((BitmapAnd *) plan)->bitmapplans));
5617	else if (IsA(plan, BitmapOr))
5618	{
5619		((BitmapOr *) plan)->isshared = true;
5620		bitmap_subplan_mark_shared(linitial(((BitmapOr *) plan)->bitmapplans));
5621	}
5622	else if (IsA(plan, BitmapIndexScan))
5623		((BitmapIndexScan *) plan)->isshared = true;
5624	else
5625		elog(ERROR, "unrecognized node type: %d", nodeTag(plan));
5626}
5627
5628/*****************************************************************************
5629 *
5630 *	PLAN NODE BUILDING ROUTINES
5631 *
5632 * In general, these functions are not passed the original Path and therefore
5633 * leave it to the caller to fill in the cost/width fields from the Path,
5634 * typically by calling copy_generic_path_info().  This convention is
5635 * somewhat historical, but it does support a few places above where we build
5636 * a plan node without having an exactly corresponding Path node.  Under no
5637 * circumstances should one of these functions do its own cost calculations,
5638 * as that would be redundant with calculations done while building Paths.
5639 *
5640 *****************************************************************************/
5641
5642static SeqScan *
5643make_seqscan(List *qptlist,
5644			 List *qpqual,
5645			 Index scanrelid)
5646{
5647	SeqScan    *node = makeNode(SeqScan);
5648	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5649
5650	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5651	plan->qual = qpqual;
5652	plan->lefttree = NULL;
5653	plan->righttree = NULL;
5654	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5655
5656	return node;
5657}
5658
5659static SampleScan *
5660make_samplescan(List *qptlist,
5661				List *qpqual,
5662				Index scanrelid,
5663				TableSampleClause *tsc)
5664{
5665	SampleScan *node = makeNode(SampleScan);
5666	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5667
5668	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5669	plan->qual = qpqual;
5670	plan->lefttree = NULL;
5671	plan->righttree = NULL;
5672	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5673	node->tablesample = tsc;
5674
5675	return node;
5676}
5677
5678static IndexScan *
5679make_indexscan(List *qptlist,
5680			   List *qpqual,
5681			   Index scanrelid,
5682			   Oid indexid,
5683			   List *indexqual,
5684			   List *indexqualorig,
5685			   List *indexorderby,
5686			   List *indexorderbyorig,
5687			   List *indexorderbyops,
5688			   ScanDirection indexscandir)
5689{
5690	IndexScan  *node = makeNode(IndexScan);
5691	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5692
5693	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5694	plan->qual = qpqual;
5695	plan->lefttree = NULL;
5696	plan->righttree = NULL;
5697	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5698	node->indexid = indexid;
5699	node->indexqual = indexqual;
5700	node->indexqualorig = indexqualorig;
5701	node->indexorderby = indexorderby;
5702	node->indexorderbyorig = indexorderbyorig;
5703	node->indexorderbyops = indexorderbyops;
5704	node->indexorderdir = indexscandir;
5705
5706	return node;
5707}
5708
5709static IndexOnlyScan *
5710make_indexonlyscan(List *qptlist,
5711				   List *qpqual,
5712				   Index scanrelid,
5713				   Oid indexid,
5714				   List *indexqual,
5715				   List *recheckqual,
5716				   List *indexorderby,
5717				   List *indextlist,
5718				   ScanDirection indexscandir)
5719{
5720	IndexOnlyScan *node = makeNode(IndexOnlyScan);
5721	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5722
5723	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5724	plan->qual = qpqual;
5725	plan->lefttree = NULL;
5726	plan->righttree = NULL;
5727	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5728	node->indexid = indexid;
5729	node->indexqual = indexqual;
5730	node->recheckqual = recheckqual;
5731	node->indexorderby = indexorderby;
5732	node->indextlist = indextlist;
5733	node->indexorderdir = indexscandir;
5734
5735	return node;
5736}
5737
5738static BitmapIndexScan *
5739make_bitmap_indexscan(Index scanrelid,
5740					  Oid indexid,
5741					  List *indexqual,
5742					  List *indexqualorig)
5743{
5744	BitmapIndexScan *node = makeNode(BitmapIndexScan);
5745	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5746
5747	plan->targetlist = NIL;		/* not used */
5748	plan->qual = NIL;			/* not used */
5749	plan->lefttree = NULL;
5750	plan->righttree = NULL;
5751	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5752	node->indexid = indexid;
5753	node->indexqual = indexqual;
5754	node->indexqualorig = indexqualorig;
5755
5756	return node;
5757}
5758
5759static BitmapHeapScan *
5760make_bitmap_heapscan(List *qptlist,
5761					 List *qpqual,
5762					 Plan *lefttree,
5763					 List *bitmapqualorig,
5764					 Index scanrelid)
5765{
5766	BitmapHeapScan *node = makeNode(BitmapHeapScan);
5767	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5768
5769	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5770	plan->qual = qpqual;
5771	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
5772	plan->righttree = NULL;
5773	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5774	node->bitmapqualorig = bitmapqualorig;
5775
5776	return node;
5777}
5778

5780make_tidscan(List *qptlist,
5781			 List *qpqual,
5782			 Index scanrelid,
5783			 List *tidquals)
5784{
5785	TidScan    *node = makeNode(TidScan);
5786	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5787
5788	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5789	plan->qual = qpqual;
5790	plan->lefttree = NULL;
5791	plan->righttree = NULL;
5792	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5793	node->tidquals = tidquals;
5794
5795	return node;
5796}
5797
5798static TidRangeScan *
5799make_tidrangescan(List *qptlist,
5800				  List *qpqual,
5801				  Index scanrelid,
5802				  List *tidrangequals)
5803{
5804	TidRangeScan *node = makeNode(TidRangeScan);
5805	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5806
5807	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5808	plan->qual = qpqual;
5809	plan->lefttree = NULL;
5810	plan->righttree = NULL;
5811	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5812	node->tidrangequals = tidrangequals;
5813
5814	return node;
5815}
5816
5817static SubqueryScan *
5818make_subqueryscan(List *qptlist,
5819				  List *qpqual,
5820				  Index scanrelid,
5821				  Plan *subplan)
5822{
5823	SubqueryScan *node = makeNode(SubqueryScan);
5824	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5825
5826	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5827	plan->qual = qpqual;
5828	plan->lefttree = NULL;
5829	plan->righttree = NULL;
5830	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5831	node->subplan = subplan;
5832	node->scanstatus = SUBQUERY_SCAN_UNKNOWN;
5833
5834	return node;
5835}
5836
5837static FunctionScan *
5838make_functionscan(List *qptlist,
5839				  List *qpqual,
5840				  Index scanrelid,
5841				  List *functions,
5842				  bool funcordinality)
5843{
5844	FunctionScan *node = makeNode(FunctionScan);
5845	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5846
5847	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5848	plan->qual = qpqual;
5849	plan->lefttree = NULL;
5850	plan->righttree = NULL;
5851	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5852	node->functions = functions;
5853	node->funcordinality = funcordinality;
5854
5855	return node;
5856}
