5858static TableFuncScan *
5859make_tablefuncscan(List *qptlist,
5860				   List *qpqual,
5861				   Index scanrelid,
5862				   TableFunc *tablefunc)
5863{
5864	TableFuncScan *node = makeNode(TableFuncScan);
5865	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5866
5867	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5868	plan->qual = qpqual;
5869	plan->lefttree = NULL;
5870	plan->righttree = NULL;
5871	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5872	node->tablefunc = tablefunc;
5873
5874	return node;
5875}
5876
5877static ValuesScan *
5878make_valuesscan(List *qptlist,
5879				List *qpqual,
5880				Index scanrelid,
5881				List *values_lists)
5882{
5883	ValuesScan *node = makeNode(ValuesScan);
5884	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5885
5886	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5887	plan->qual = qpqual;
5888	plan->lefttree = NULL;
5889	plan->righttree = NULL;
5890	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5891	node->values_lists = values_lists;
5892
5893	return node;
5894}
5895
5896static CteScan *
5897make_ctescan(List *qptlist,
5898			 List *qpqual,
5899			 Index scanrelid,
5900			 int ctePlanId,
5901			 int cteParam)
5902{
5903	CteScan    *node = makeNode(CteScan);
5904	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5905
5906	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5907	plan->qual = qpqual;
5908	plan->lefttree = NULL;
5909	plan->righttree = NULL;
5910	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5911	node->ctePlanId = ctePlanId;
5912	node->cteParam = cteParam;
5913
5914	return node;
5915}
5916
5917static NamedTuplestoreScan *
5918make_namedtuplestorescan(List *qptlist,
5919						 List *qpqual,
5920						 Index scanrelid,
5921						 char *enrname)
5922{
5923	NamedTuplestoreScan *node = makeNode(NamedTuplestoreScan);
5924	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5925
5926	/* cost should be inserted by caller */
5927	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5928	plan->qual = qpqual;
5929	plan->lefttree = NULL;
5930	plan->righttree = NULL;
5931	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5932	node->enrname = enrname;
5933
5934	return node;
5935}
5936
5937static WorkTableScan *
5938make_worktablescan(List *qptlist,
5939				   List *qpqual,
5940				   Index scanrelid,
5941				   int wtParam)
5942{
5943	WorkTableScan *node = makeNode(WorkTableScan);
5944	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5945
5946	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5947	plan->qual = qpqual;
5948	plan->lefttree = NULL;
5949	plan->righttree = NULL;
5950	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5951	node->wtParam = wtParam;
5952
5953	return node;
5954}
5955
5956ForeignScan *
5957make_foreignscan(List *qptlist,
5958				 List *qpqual,
5959				 Index scanrelid,
5960				 List *fdw_exprs,
5961				 List *fdw_private,
5962				 List *fdw_scan_tlist,
5963				 List *fdw_recheck_quals,
5964				 Plan *outer_plan)
5965{
5966	ForeignScan *node = makeNode(ForeignScan);
5967	Plan	   *plan = &node->scan.plan;
5968
5969	/* cost will be filled in by create_foreignscan_plan */
5970	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
5971	plan->qual = qpqual;
5972	plan->lefttree = outer_plan;
5973	plan->righttree = NULL;
5974	node->scan.scanrelid = scanrelid;
5975
5976	/* these may be overridden by the FDW's PlanDirectModify callback. */
5977	node->operation = CMD_SELECT;
5978	node->resultRelation = 0;
5979
5980	/* checkAsUser, fs_server will be filled in by create_foreignscan_plan */
5981	node->checkAsUser = InvalidOid;
5982	node->fs_server = InvalidOid;
5983	node->fdw_exprs = fdw_exprs;
5984	node->fdw_private = fdw_private;
5985	node->fdw_scan_tlist = fdw_scan_tlist;
5986	node->fdw_recheck_quals = fdw_recheck_quals;
5987	/* fs_relids, fs_base_relids will be filled by create_foreignscan_plan */
5988	node->fs_relids = NULL;
5989	node->fs_base_relids = NULL;
5990	/* fsSystemCol will be filled in by create_foreignscan_plan */
5991	node->fsSystemCol = false;
5992
5993	return node;
5994}
5995
5996static RecursiveUnion *
5997make_recursive_union(List *tlist,
5998					 Plan *lefttree,
5999					 Plan *righttree,
6000					 int wtParam,
6001					 List *distinctList,
6002					 long numGroups)
6003{
6004	RecursiveUnion *node = makeNode(RecursiveUnion);
6005	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
6006	int			numCols = list_length(distinctList);
6007
6008	plan->targetlist = tlist;
6009	plan->qual = NIL;
6010	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
6011	plan->righttree = righttree;
6012	node->wtParam = wtParam;
6013
6014	/*
6015	 * convert SortGroupClause list into arrays of attr indexes and equality
6016	 * operators, as wanted by executor
6017	 */
6018	node->numCols = numCols;
6019	if (numCols > 0)
6020	{
6021		int			keyno = 0;
6022		AttrNumber *dupColIdx;
6023		Oid		   *dupOperators;
6024		Oid		   *dupCollations;
6025		ListCell   *slitem;
6026
6027		dupColIdx = (AttrNumber *) palloc(sizeof(AttrNumber) * numCols);
6028		dupOperators = (Oid *) palloc(sizeof(Oid) * numCols);
6029		dupCollations = (Oid *) palloc(sizeof(Oid) * numCols);
6030
6031		foreach(slitem, distinctList)
6032		{
6033			SortGroupClause *sortcl = (SortGroupClause *) lfirst(slitem);
6034			TargetEntry *tle = get_sortgroupclause_tle(sortcl,
6035													   plan->targetlist);
6036
6037			dupColIdx[keyno] = tle->resno;
6038			dupOperators[keyno] = sortcl->eqop;
6039			dupCollations[keyno] = exprCollation((Node *) tle->expr);
6040			Assert(OidIsValid(dupOperators[keyno]));
6041			keyno++;
6042		}
6043		node->dupColIdx = dupColIdx;
6044		node->dupOperators = dupOperators;
6045		node->dupCollations = dupCollations;
6046	}
6047	node->numGroups = numGroups;
6048
6049	return node;
6050}
6051
6052static BitmapAnd *
6053make_bitmap_and(List *bitmapplans)
6054{
6055	BitmapAnd  *node = makeNode(BitmapAnd);
6056	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
6057
6058	plan->targetlist = NIL;
6059	plan->qual = NIL;
6060	plan->lefttree = NULL;
6061	plan->righttree = NULL;
6062	node->bitmapplans = bitmapplans;
6063
6064	return node;
6065}
6066
6067static BitmapOr *
6068make_bitmap_or(List *bitmapplans)
6069{
6070	BitmapOr   *node = makeNode(BitmapOr);
6071	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
6072
6073	plan->targetlist = NIL;
6074	plan->qual = NIL;
6075	plan->lefttree = NULL;
6076	plan->righttree = NULL;
6077	node->bitmapplans = bitmapplans;
6078
6079	return node;
6080}
6081
6082static NestLoop *
6083make_nestloop(List *tlist,
6084			  List *joinclauses,
6085			  List *otherclauses,
6086			  List *nestParams,
6087			  Plan *lefttree,
6088			  Plan *righttree,
6089			  JoinType jointype,
6090			  bool inner_unique)
6091{
6092	NestLoop   *node = makeNode(NestLoop);
6093	Plan	   *plan = &node->join.plan;
6094
6095	plan->targetlist = tlist;
6096	plan->qual = otherclauses;
6097	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
6098	plan->righttree = righttree;
6099	node->join.jointype = jointype;
6100	node->join.inner_unique = inner_unique;
6101	node->join.joinqual = joinclauses;
6102	node->nestParams = nestParams;
6103
6104	return node;
6105}
6106
6107static HashJoin *
6108make_hashjoin(List *tlist,
6109			  List *joinclauses,
6110			  List *otherclauses,
6111			  List *hashclauses,
6112			  List *hashoperators,
6113			  List *hashcollations,
6114			  List *hashkeys,
6115			  Plan *lefttree,
6116			  Plan *righttree,
6117			  JoinType jointype,
6118			  bool inner_unique)
6119{
6120	HashJoin   *node = makeNode(HashJoin);
6121	Plan	   *plan = &node->join.plan;
6122
6123	plan->targetlist = tlist;
6124	plan->qual = otherclauses;
6125	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
6126	plan->righttree = righttree;
6127	node->hashclauses = hashclauses;
6128	node->hashoperators = hashoperators;
6129	node->hashcollations = hashcollations;
6130	node->hashkeys = hashkeys;
6131	node->join.jointype = jointype;
6132	node->join.inner_unique = inner_unique;
6133	node->join.joinqual = joinclauses;
6134
6135	return node;
6136}
6137
6138static Hash *
6139make_hash(Plan *lefttree,
6140		  List *hashkeys,
6141		  Oid skewTable,
6142		  AttrNumber skewColumn,
6143		  bool skewInherit)
6144{
6145	Hash	   *node = makeNode(Hash);
6146	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
6147
6148	plan->targetlist = lefttree->targetlist;
6149	plan->qual = NIL;
6150	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
6151	plan->righttree = NULL;
6152
6153	node->hashkeys = hashkeys;
6154	node->skewTable = skewTable;
6155	node->skewColumn = skewColumn;
6156	node->skewInherit = skewInherit;
6157
6158	return node;
6159}
6160
6161static MergeJoin *
6162make_mergejoin(List *tlist,
6163			   List *joinclauses,
6164			   List *otherclauses,
6165			   List *mergeclauses,
6166			   Oid *mergefamilies,
6167			   Oid *mergecollations,
6168			   bool *mergereversals,
6169			   bool *mergenullsfirst,
6170			   Plan *lefttree,
6171			   Plan *righttree,
6172			   JoinType jointype,
6173			   bool inner_unique,
6174			   bool skip_mark_restore)
6175{
6176	MergeJoin  *node = makeNode(MergeJoin);
6177	Plan	   *plan = &node->join.plan;
6178
6179	plan->targetlist = tlist;
6180	plan->qual = otherclauses;
6181	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
6182	plan->righttree = righttree;
6183	node->skip_mark_restore = skip_mark_restore;
6184	node->mergeclauses = mergeclauses;
6185	node->mergeFamilies = mergefamilies;
6186	node->mergeCollations = mergecollations;
6187	node->mergeReversals = mergereversals;
6188	node->mergeNullsFirst = mergenullsfirst;
6189	node->join.jointype = jointype;
6190	node->join.inner_unique = inner_unique;
6191	node->join.joinqual = joinclauses;
6192
6193	return node;
6194}
6195
6196/*
6197 * make_sort --- basic routine to build a Sort plan node
6198 *
6199 * Caller must have built the sortColIdx, sortOperators, collations, and
6200 * nullsFirst arrays already.
6201 */
6202static Sort *
6203make_sort(Plan *lefttree, int numCols,
6204		  AttrNumber *sortColIdx, Oid *sortOperators,
6205		  Oid *collations, bool *nullsFirst)
6206{
6207	Sort	   *node;
6208	Plan	   *plan;
6209
6210	node = makeNode(Sort);
6211
6212	plan = &node->plan;
6213	plan->targetlist = lefttree->targetlist;
6214	plan->disabled_nodes = lefttree->disabled_nodes + (enable_sort == false);
6215	plan->qual = NIL;
6216	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
6217	plan->righttree = NULL;
6218	node->numCols = numCols;
6219	node->sortColIdx = sortColIdx;
6220	node->sortOperators = sortOperators;
6221	node->collations = collations;
6222	node->nullsFirst = nullsFirst;
6223
6224	return node;
6225}
6226
6227/*
6228 * make_incrementalsort --- basic routine to build an IncrementalSort plan node
6229 *
6230 * Caller must have built the sortColIdx, sortOperators, collations, and
6231 * nullsFirst arrays already.
6232 */
6233static IncrementalSort *
6234make_incrementalsort(Plan *lefttree, int numCols, int nPresortedCols,
6235					 AttrNumber *sortColIdx, Oid *sortOperators,
6236					 Oid *collations, bool *nullsFirst)
6237{
6238	IncrementalSort *node;
6239	Plan	   *plan;
6240
6241	node = makeNode(IncrementalSort);
6242
6243	plan = &node->sort.plan;
6244	plan->targetlist = lefttree->targetlist;
6245	plan->qual = NIL;
6246	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
6247	plan->righttree = NULL;
6248	node->nPresortedCols = nPresortedCols;
6249	node->sort.numCols = numCols;
6250	node->sort.sortColIdx = sortColIdx;
6251	node->sort.sortOperators = sortOperators;
6252	node->sort.collations = collations;
6253	node->sort.nullsFirst = nullsFirst;
6254
6255	return node;
6256}
6257
6258/*
6259 * prepare_sort_from_pathkeys
6260 *	  Prepare to sort according to given pathkeys
6261 *
6262 * This is used to set up for Sort, MergeAppend, and Gather Merge nodes.  It
6263 * calculates the executor's representation of the sort key information, and
6264 * adjusts the plan targetlist if needed to add resjunk sort columns.
6265 *
6266 * Input parameters:
6267 *	  'lefttree' is the plan node which yields input tuples
6268 *	  'pathkeys' is the list of pathkeys by which the result is to be sorted
6269 *	  'relids' identifies the child relation being sorted, if any
6270 *	  'reqColIdx' is NULL or an array of required sort key column numbers
6271 *	  'adjust_tlist_in_place' is true if lefttree must be modified in-place
6272 *
6273 * We must convert the pathkey information into arrays of sort key column
6274 * numbers, sort operator OIDs, collation OIDs, and nulls-first flags,
6275 * which is the representation the executor wants.  These are returned into
6276 * the output parameters *p_numsortkeys etc.
6277 *
6278 * When looking for matches to an EquivalenceClass's members, we will only
6279 * consider child EC members if they belong to given 'relids'.  This protects
6280 * against possible incorrect matches to child expressions that contain no
6281 * Vars.
6282 *
6283 * If reqColIdx isn't NULL then it contains sort key column numbers that
6284 * we should match.  This is used when making child plans for a MergeAppend;
6285 * it's an error if we can't match the columns.
6286 *
6287 * If the pathkeys include expressions that aren't simple Vars, we will
6288 * usually need to add resjunk items to the input plan's targetlist to
6289 * compute these expressions, since a Sort or MergeAppend node itself won't
6290 * do any such calculations.  If the input plan type isn't one that can do
6291 * projections, this means adding a Result node just to do the projection.
6292 * However, the caller can pass adjust_tlist_in_place = true to force the
6293 * lefttree tlist to be modified in-place regardless of whether the node type
6294 * can project --- we use this for fixing the tlist of MergeAppend itself.
6295 *
6296 * Returns the node which is to be the input to the Sort (either lefttree,
6297 * or a Result stacked atop lefttree).
6298 */
6299static Plan *
6300prepare_sort_from_pathkeys(Plan *lefttree, List *pathkeys,
6301						   Relids relids,
6302						   const AttrNumber *reqColIdx,
6303						   bool adjust_tlist_in_place,
6304						   int *p_numsortkeys,
6305						   AttrNumber **p_sortColIdx,
6306						   Oid **p_sortOperators,
6307						   Oid **p_collations,
6308						   bool **p_nullsFirst)
6309{
6310	List	   *tlist = lefttree->targetlist;
6311	ListCell   *i;
6312	int			numsortkeys;
6313	AttrNumber *sortColIdx;
6314	Oid		   *sortOperators;
6315	Oid		   *collations;
6316	bool	   *nullsFirst;
6317
6318	/*
6319	 * We will need at most list_length(pathkeys) sort columns; possibly less
6320	 */
6321	numsortkeys = list_length(pathkeys);
6322	sortColIdx = (AttrNumber *) palloc(numsortkeys * sizeof(AttrNumber));
6323	sortOperators = (Oid *) palloc(numsortkeys * sizeof(Oid));
6324	collations = (Oid *) palloc(numsortkeys * sizeof(Oid));
6325	nullsFirst = (bool *) palloc(numsortkeys * sizeof(bool));
6326
6327	numsortkeys = 0;
6328
6329	foreach(i, pathkeys)
6330	{
6331		PathKey    *pathkey = (PathKey *) lfirst(i);
6332		EquivalenceClass *ec = pathkey->pk_eclass;
6333		EquivalenceMember *em;
6334		TargetEntry *tle = NULL;
6335		Oid			pk_datatype = InvalidOid;
6336		Oid			sortop;
6337		ListCell   *j;
6338
6339		if (ec->ec_has_volatile)
6340		{
6341			/*
6342			 * If the pathkey's EquivalenceClass is volatile, then it must
6343			 * have come from an ORDER BY clause, and we have to match it to
6344			 * that same targetlist entry.
6345			 */
6346			if (ec->ec_sortref == 0)	/* can't happen */
6347				elog(ERROR, "volatile EquivalenceClass has no sortref");
6348			tle = get_sortgroupref_tle(ec->ec_sortref, tlist);
6349			Assert(tle);
6350			Assert(list_length(ec->ec_members) == 1);
6351			pk_datatype = ((EquivalenceMember *) linitial(ec->ec_members))->em_datatype;
6352		}
6353		else if (reqColIdx != NULL)
6354		{
6355			/*
6356			 * If we are given a sort column number to match, only consider
6357			 * the single TLE at that position.  It's possible that there is
6358			 * no such TLE, in which case fall through and generate a resjunk
6359			 * targetentry (we assume this must have happened in the parent
6360			 * plan as well).  If there is a TLE but it doesn't match the
6361			 * pathkey's EC, we do the same, which is probably the wrong thing
6362			 * but we'll leave it to caller to complain about the mismatch.
6363			 */
6364			tle = get_tle_by_resno(tlist, reqColIdx[numsortkeys]);
6365			if (tle)
6366			{
6367				em = find_ec_member_matching_expr(ec, tle->expr, relids);
6368				if (em)
6369				{
6370					/* found expr at right place in tlist */
6371					pk_datatype = em->em_datatype;
6372				}
6373				else
6374					tle = NULL;
6375			}
6376		}
6377		else
6378		{
6379			/*
6380			 * Otherwise, we can sort by any non-constant expression listed in
6381			 * the pathkey's EquivalenceClass.  For now, we take the first
6382			 * tlist item found in the EC. If there's no match, we'll generate
6383			 * a resjunk entry using the first EC member that is an expression
6384			 * in the input's vars.
6385			 *
6386			 * XXX if we have a choice, is there any way of figuring out which
6387			 * might be cheapest to execute?  (For example, int4lt is likely
6388			 * much cheaper to execute than numericlt, but both might appear
6389			 * in the same equivalence class...)  Not clear that we ever will
6390			 * have an interesting choice in practice, so it may not matter.
6391			 */
6392			foreach(j, tlist)
6393			{
6394				tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(j);
6395				em = find_ec_member_matching_expr(ec, tle->expr, relids);
6396				if (em)
6397				{
6398					/* found expr already in tlist */
6399					pk_datatype = em->em_datatype;
6400					break;
6401				}
6402				tle = NULL;
6403			}
6404		}
6405
6406		if (!tle)
6407		{
6408			/*
6409			 * No matching tlist item; look for a computable expression.
6410			 */
6411			em = find_computable_ec_member(NULL, ec, tlist, relids, false);
6412			if (!em)
6413				elog(ERROR, "could not find pathkey item to sort");
6414			pk_datatype = em->em_datatype;
6415
6416			/*
6417			 * Do we need to insert a Result node?
6418			 */
6419			if (!adjust_tlist_in_place &&
6420				!is_projection_capable_plan(lefttree))
6421			{
6422				/* copy needed so we don't modify input's tlist below */
6423				tlist = copyObject(tlist);
6424				lefttree = inject_projection_plan(lefttree, tlist,
6425												  lefttree->parallel_safe);
6426			}
6427
6428			/* Don't bother testing is_projection_capable_plan again */
6429			adjust_tlist_in_place = true;
6430
6431			/*
6432			 * Add resjunk entry to input's tlist
6433			 */
6434			tle = makeTargetEntry(copyObject(em->em_expr),
6435								  list_length(tlist) + 1,
6436								  NULL,
6437								  true);
6438			tlist = lappend(tlist, tle);
6439			lefttree->targetlist = tlist;	/* just in case NIL before */
6440		}
6441
6442		/*
6443		 * Look up the correct sort operator from the PathKey's slightly
6444		 * abstracted representation.
6445		 */
6446		sortop = get_opfamily_member_for_cmptype(pathkey->pk_opfamily,
6447												 pk_datatype,
6448												 pk_datatype,
6449												 pathkey->pk_cmptype);
6450		if (!OidIsValid(sortop))	/* should not happen */
6451			elog(ERROR, "missing operator %d(%u,%u) in opfamily %u",
6452				 pathkey->pk_cmptype, pk_datatype, pk_datatype,
6453				 pathkey->pk_opfamily);
6454
6455		/* Add the column to the sort arrays */
6456		sortColIdx[numsortkeys] = tle->resno;
6457		sortOperators[numsortkeys] = sortop;
6458		collations[numsortkeys] = ec->ec_collation;
6459		nullsFirst[numsortkeys] = pathkey->pk_nulls_first;
6460		numsortkeys++;
6461	}
6462
6463	/* Return results */
6464	*p_numsortkeys = numsortkeys;
6465	*p_sortColIdx = sortColIdx;
6466	*p_sortOperators = sortOperators;
6467	*p_collations = collations;
6468	*p_nullsFirst = nullsFirst;
6469
6470	return lefttree;
6471}
6472
6473/*
6474 * make_sort_from_pathkeys
6475 *	  Create sort plan to sort according to given pathkeys
6476 *
6477 *	  'lefttree' is the node which yields input tuples
6478 *	  'pathkeys' is the list of pathkeys by which the result is to be sorted
6479 *	  'relids' is the set of relations required by prepare_sort_from_pathkeys()
6480 */
6481static Sort *
6482make_sort_from_pathkeys(Plan *lefttree, List *pathkeys, Relids relids)
6483{
6484	int			numsortkeys;
6485	AttrNumber *sortColIdx;
6486	Oid		   *sortOperators;
6487	Oid		   *collations;
6488	bool	   *nullsFirst;
6489
6490	/* Compute sort column info, and adjust lefttree as needed */
6491	lefttree = prepare_sort_from_pathkeys(lefttree, pathkeys,
6492										  relids,
6493										  NULL,
6494										  false,
6495										  &numsortkeys,
6496										  &sortColIdx,
6497										  &sortOperators,
6498										  &collations,
6499										  &nullsFirst);
6500
6501	/* Now build the Sort node */
6502	return make_sort(lefttree, numsortkeys,
6503					 sortColIdx, sortOperators,
6504					 collations, nullsFirst);
6505}
6506
6507/*
6508 * make_incrementalsort_from_pathkeys
6509 *	  Create sort plan to sort according to given pathkeys
6510 *
6511 *	  'lefttree' is the node which yields input tuples
6512 *	  'pathkeys' is the list of pathkeys by which the result is to be sorted
6513 *	  'relids' is the set of relations required by prepare_sort_from_pathkeys()
6514 *	  'nPresortedCols' is the number of presorted columns in input tuples
6515 */
6516static IncrementalSort *
6517make_incrementalsort_from_pathkeys(Plan *lefttree, List *pathkeys,
6518								   Relids relids, int nPresortedCols)
6519{
6520	int			numsortkeys;
6521	AttrNumber *sortColIdx;
6522	Oid		   *sortOperators;
6523	Oid		   *collations;
6524	bool	   *nullsFirst;
6525
6526	/* Compute sort column info, and adjust lefttree as needed */
6527	lefttree = prepare_sort_from_pathkeys(lefttree, pathkeys,
6528										  relids,
6529										  NULL,
6530										  false,
6531										  &numsortkeys,
6532										  &sortColIdx,
6533										  &sortOperators,
6534										  &collations,
6535										  &nullsFirst);
6536
6537	/* Now build the Sort node */
6538	return make_incrementalsort(lefttree, numsortkeys, nPresortedCols,
6539								sortColIdx, sortOperators,
6540								collations, nullsFirst);
6541}
6542
6543/*
6544 * make_sort_from_sortclauses
6545 *	  Create sort plan to sort according to given sortclauses
6546 *
6547 *	  'sortcls' is a list of SortGroupClauses
6548 *	  'lefttree' is the node which yields input tuples
6549 */
6550Sort *
6551make_sort_from_sortclauses(List *sortcls, Plan *lefttree)
6552{
6553	List	   *sub_tlist = lefttree->targetlist;
6554	ListCell   *l;
6555	int			numsortkeys;
6556	AttrNumber *sortColIdx;
6557	Oid		   *sortOperators;
6558	Oid		   *collations;
6559	bool	   *nullsFirst;
6560
6561	/* Convert list-ish representation to arrays wanted by executor */
6562	numsortkeys = list_length(sortcls);
6563	sortColIdx = (AttrNumber *) palloc(numsortkeys * sizeof(AttrNumber));
6564	sortOperators = (Oid *) palloc(numsortkeys * sizeof(Oid));
6565	collations = (Oid *) palloc(numsortkeys * sizeof(Oid));
6566	nullsFirst = (bool *) palloc(numsortkeys * sizeof(bool));
6567
6568	numsortkeys = 0;
6569	foreach(l, sortcls)
6570	{
6571		SortGroupClause *sortcl = (SortGroupClause *) lfirst(l);
6572		TargetEntry *tle = get_sortgroupclause_tle(sortcl, sub_tlist);
6573
6574		sortColIdx[numsortkeys] = tle->resno;
6575		sortOperators[numsortkeys] = sortcl->sortop;
6576		collations[numsortkeys] = exprCollation((Node *) tle->expr);
6577		nullsFirst[numsortkeys] = sortcl->nulls_first;
6578		numsortkeys++;
6579	}
6580
6581	return make_sort(lefttree, numsortkeys,
6582					 sortColIdx, sortOperators,
6583					 collations, nullsFirst);
6584}
6585
6586/*
6587 * make_sort_from_groupcols
6588 *	  Create sort plan to sort based on grouping columns
6589 *
6590 * 'groupcls' is the list of SortGroupClauses
6591 * 'grpColIdx' gives the column numbers to use
6592 *
6593 * This might look like it could be merged with make_sort_from_sortclauses,
6594 * but presently we *must* use the grpColIdx[] array to locate sort columns,
6595 * because the child plan's tlist is not marked with ressortgroupref info
6596 * appropriate to the grouping node.  So, only the sort ordering info
6597 * is used from the SortGroupClause entries.
6598 */
6599static Sort *
6600make_sort_from_groupcols(List *groupcls,
6601						 AttrNumber *grpColIdx,
6602						 Plan *lefttree)
6603{
6604	List	   *sub_tlist = lefttree->targetlist;
6605	ListCell   *l;
6606	int			numsortkeys;
6607	AttrNumber *sortColIdx;
6608	Oid		   *sortOperators;
6609	Oid		   *collations;
6610	bool	   *nullsFirst;
6611
6612	/* Convert list-ish representation to arrays wanted by executor */
6613	numsortkeys = list_length(groupcls);
6614	sortColIdx = (AttrNumber *) palloc(numsortkeys * sizeof(AttrNumber));
6615	sortOperators = (Oid *) palloc(numsortkeys * sizeof(Oid));
6616	collations = (Oid *) palloc(numsortkeys * sizeof(Oid));
6617	nullsFirst = (bool *) palloc(numsortkeys * sizeof(bool));
6618
6619	numsortkeys = 0;
6620	foreach(l, groupcls)
6621	{
6622		SortGroupClause *grpcl = (SortGroupClause *) lfirst(l);
6623		TargetEntry *tle = get_tle_by_resno(sub_tlist, grpColIdx[numsortkeys]);
6624
6625		if (!tle)
6626			elog(ERROR, "could not retrieve tle for sort-from-groupcols");
6627
6628		sortColIdx[numsortkeys] = tle->resno;
6629		sortOperators[numsortkeys] = grpcl->sortop;
6630		collations[numsortkeys] = exprCollation((Node *) tle->expr);
6631		nullsFirst[numsortkeys] = grpcl->nulls_first;
6632		numsortkeys++;
6633	}
6634
6635	return make_sort(lefttree, numsortkeys,
6636					 sortColIdx, sortOperators,
6637					 collations, nullsFirst);
6638}
6639
6640static Material *
6641make_material(Plan *lefttree)
6642{
6643	Material   *node = makeNode(Material);
6644	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
6645
6646	plan->targetlist = lefttree->targetlist;
6647	plan->qual = NIL;
6648	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
6649	plan->righttree = NULL;
6650
6651	return node;
6652}
6653
6654/*
6655 * materialize_finished_plan: stick a Material node atop a completed plan
6656 *
6657 * There are a couple of places where we want to attach a Material node
6658 * after completion of create_plan(), without any MaterialPath path.
6659 * Those places should probably be refactored someday to do this on the
6660 * Path representation, but it's not worth the trouble yet.
6661 */
6662Plan *
6663materialize_finished_plan(Plan *subplan)
6664{
6665	Plan	   *matplan;
6666	Path		matpath;		/* dummy for result of cost_material */
6667	Cost		initplan_cost;
6668	bool		unsafe_initplans;
6669
6670	matplan = (Plan *) make_material(subplan);
6671
6672	/*
6673	 * XXX horrid kluge: if there are any initPlans attached to the subplan,
6674	 * move them up to the Material node, which is now effectively the top
6675	 * plan node in its query level.  This prevents failure in
6676	 * SS_finalize_plan(), which see for comments.
6677	 */
6678	matplan->initPlan = subplan->initPlan;
6679	subplan->initPlan = NIL;
6680
6681	/* Move the initplans' cost delta, as well */
6682	SS_compute_initplan_cost(matplan->initPlan,
6683							 &initplan_cost, &unsafe_initplans);
6684	subplan->startup_cost -= initplan_cost;
6685	subplan->total_cost -= initplan_cost;
6686
6687	/* Set cost data */
6688	cost_material(&matpath,
6689				  subplan->disabled_nodes,
6690				  subplan->startup_cost,
6691				  subplan->total_cost,
6692				  subplan->plan_rows,
6693				  subplan->plan_width);
6694	matplan->disabled_nodes = subplan->disabled_nodes;
6695	matplan->startup_cost = matpath.startup_cost + initplan_cost;
6696	matplan->total_cost = matpath.total_cost + initplan_cost;
6697	matplan->plan_rows = subplan->plan_rows;
6698	matplan->plan_width = subplan->plan_width;
6699	matplan->parallel_aware = false;
6700	matplan->parallel_safe = subplan->parallel_safe;
6701
6702	return matplan;
6703}
6704
6705static Memoize *
6706make_memoize(Plan *lefttree, Oid *hashoperators, Oid *collations,
6707			 List *param_exprs, bool singlerow, bool binary_mode,
6708			 uint32 est_entries, Bitmapset *keyparamids)
6709{
6710	Memoize    *node = makeNode(Memoize);
6711	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
6712
6713	plan->targetlist = lefttree->targetlist;
6714	plan->qual = NIL;
6715	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
6716	plan->righttree = NULL;
6717
6718	node->numKeys = list_length(param_exprs);
6719	node->hashOperators = hashoperators;
6720	node->collations = collations;
6721	node->param_exprs = param_exprs;
6722	node->singlerow = singlerow;
6723	node->binary_mode = binary_mode;
6724	node->est_entries = est_entries;
6725	node->keyparamids = keyparamids;
6726
6727	return node;
6728}
6729
6730Agg *
6731make_agg(List *tlist, List *qual,
6732		 AggStrategy aggstrategy, AggSplit aggsplit,
6733		 int numGroupCols, AttrNumber *grpColIdx, Oid *grpOperators, Oid *grpCollations,
6734		 List *groupingSets, List *chain, double dNumGroups,
6735		 Size transitionSpace, Plan *lefttree)
6736{
6737	Agg		   *node = makeNode(Agg);
6738	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
6739	long		numGroups;
6740
6741	/* Reduce to long, but 'ware overflow! */
6742	numGroups = clamp_cardinality_to_long(dNumGroups);
6743
6744	node->aggstrategy = aggstrategy;
6745	node->aggsplit = aggsplit;
6746	node->numCols = numGroupCols;
6747	node->grpColIdx = grpColIdx;
6748	node->grpOperators = grpOperators;
6749	node->grpCollations = grpCollations;
6750	node->numGroups = numGroups;
6751	node->transitionSpace = transitionSpace;
6752	node->aggParams = NULL;		/* SS_finalize_plan() will fill this */
6753	node->groupingSets = groupingSets;
6754	node->chain = chain;
6755
6756	plan->qual = qual;
6757	plan->targetlist = tlist;
6758	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
6759	plan->righttree = NULL;
6760
6761	return node;
6762}
6763
6764static WindowAgg *
6765make_windowagg(List *tlist, WindowClause *wc,
6766			   int partNumCols, AttrNumber *partColIdx, Oid *partOperators, Oid *partCollations,
6767			   int ordNumCols, AttrNumber *ordColIdx, Oid *ordOperators, Oid *ordCollations,
6768			   List *runCondition, List *qual, bool topWindow, Plan *lefttree)
6769{
6770	WindowAgg  *node = makeNode(WindowAgg);
6771	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
6772
6773	node->winname = wc->name;
6774	node->winref = wc->winref;
6775	node->partNumCols = partNumCols;
6776	node->partColIdx = partColIdx;
6777	node->partOperators = partOperators;
6778	node->partCollations = partCollations;
6779	node->ordNumCols = ordNumCols;
6780	node->ordColIdx = ordColIdx;
6781	node->ordOperators = ordOperators;
6782	node->ordCollations = ordCollations;
6783	node->frameOptions = wc->frameOptions;
6784	node->startOffset = wc->startOffset;
6785	node->endOffset = wc->endOffset;
6786	node->runCondition = runCondition;
6787	/* a duplicate of the above for EXPLAIN */
6788	node->runConditionOrig = runCondition;
6789	node->startInRangeFunc = wc->startInRangeFunc;
6790	node->endInRangeFunc = wc->endInRangeFunc;
6791	node->inRangeColl = wc->inRangeColl;
6792	node->inRangeAsc = wc->inRangeAsc;
6793	node->inRangeNullsFirst = wc->inRangeNullsFirst;
6794	node->topWindow = topWindow;
6795
6796	plan->targetlist = tlist;
6797	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
6798	plan->righttree = NULL;
6799	plan->qual = qual;
6800
6801	return node;
6802}
6803
6804static Group *
6805make_group(List *tlist,
6806		   List *qual,
6807		   int numGroupCols,
6808		   AttrNumber *grpColIdx,
6809		   Oid *grpOperators,
6810		   Oid *grpCollations,
6811		   Plan *lefttree)
6812{
6813	Group	   *node = makeNode(Group);
6814	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
6815
6816	node->numCols = numGroupCols;
6817	node->grpColIdx = grpColIdx;
6818	node->grpOperators = grpOperators;
6819	node->grpCollations = grpCollations;
6820
6821	plan->qual = qual;
6822	plan->targetlist = tlist;
6823	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
6824	plan->righttree = NULL;
6825
6826	return node;
6827}
6828
6829/*
6830 * distinctList is a list of SortGroupClauses, identifying the targetlist items
6831 * that should be considered by the Unique filter.  The input path must
6832 * already be sorted accordingly.
6833 */
6834static Unique *
6835make_unique_from_sortclauses(Plan *lefttree, List *distinctList)
6836{
6837	Unique	   *node = makeNode(Unique);
6838	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
6839	int			numCols = list_length(distinctList);
6840	int			keyno = 0;
6841	AttrNumber *uniqColIdx;
6842	Oid		   *uniqOperators;
6843	Oid		   *uniqCollations;
6844	ListCell   *slitem;
6845
6846	plan->targetlist = lefttree->targetlist;
6847	plan->qual = NIL;
6848	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
6849	plan->righttree = NULL;
6850
6851	/*
6852	 * convert SortGroupClause list into arrays of attr indexes and equality
6853	 * operators, as wanted by executor
6854	 */
6855	Assert(numCols > 0);
6856	uniqColIdx = (AttrNumber *) palloc(sizeof(AttrNumber) * numCols);
6857	uniqOperators = (Oid *) palloc(sizeof(Oid) * numCols);
6858	uniqCollations = (Oid *) palloc(sizeof(Oid) * numCols);
6859
6860	foreach(slitem, distinctList)
6861	{
6862		SortGroupClause *sortcl = (SortGroupClause *) lfirst(slitem);
6863		TargetEntry *tle = get_sortgroupclause_tle(sortcl, plan->targetlist);
6864
6865		uniqColIdx[keyno] = tle->resno;
6866		uniqOperators[keyno] = sortcl->eqop;
6867		uniqCollations[keyno] = exprCollation((Node *) tle->expr);
6868		Assert(OidIsValid(uniqOperators[keyno]));
6869		keyno++;
6870	}
6871
6872	node->numCols = numCols;
6873	node->uniqColIdx = uniqColIdx;
6874	node->uniqOperators = uniqOperators;
6875	node->uniqCollations = uniqCollations;
6876
6877	return node;
6878}
6879
6880/*
6881 * as above, but use pathkeys to identify the sort columns and semantics
6882 */
6883static Unique *
6884make_unique_from_pathkeys(Plan *lefttree, List *pathkeys, int numCols)
6885{
6886	Unique	   *node = makeNode(Unique);
6887	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
6888	int			keyno = 0;
6889	AttrNumber *uniqColIdx;
6890	Oid		   *uniqOperators;
6891	Oid		   *uniqCollations;
6892	ListCell   *lc;
6893
6894	plan->targetlist = lefttree->targetlist;
6895	plan->qual = NIL;
6896	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
6897	plan->righttree = NULL;
6898
6899	/*
6900	 * Convert pathkeys list into arrays of attr indexes and equality
6901	 * operators, as wanted by executor.  This has a lot in common with
6902	 * prepare_sort_from_pathkeys ... maybe unify sometime?
6903	 */
6904	Assert(numCols >= 0 && numCols <= list_length(pathkeys));
6905	uniqColIdx = (AttrNumber *) palloc(sizeof(AttrNumber) * numCols);
6906	uniqOperators = (Oid *) palloc(sizeof(Oid) * numCols);
6907	uniqCollations = (Oid *) palloc(sizeof(Oid) * numCols);
6908
6909	foreach(lc, pathkeys)
6910	{
6911		PathKey    *pathkey = (PathKey *) lfirst(lc);
6912		EquivalenceClass *ec = pathkey->pk_eclass;
6913		EquivalenceMember *em;
6914		TargetEntry *tle = NULL;
6915		Oid			pk_datatype = InvalidOid;
6916		Oid			eqop;
6917		ListCell   *j;
6918
6919		/* Ignore pathkeys beyond the specified number of columns */
6920		if (keyno >= numCols)
6921			break;
6922
6923		if (ec->ec_has_volatile)
6924		{
6925			/*
6926			 * If the pathkey's EquivalenceClass is volatile, then it must
6927			 * have come from an ORDER BY clause, and we have to match it to
6928			 * that same targetlist entry.
6929			 */
6930			if (ec->ec_sortref == 0)	/* can't happen */
6931				elog(ERROR, "volatile EquivalenceClass has no sortref");
6932			tle = get_sortgroupref_tle(ec->ec_sortref, plan->targetlist);
6933			Assert(tle);
6934			Assert(list_length(ec->ec_members) == 1);
6935			pk_datatype = ((EquivalenceMember *) linitial(ec->ec_members))->em_datatype;
6936		}
6937		else
6938		{
6939			/*
6940			 * Otherwise, we can use any non-constant expression listed in the
6941			 * pathkey's EquivalenceClass.  For now, we take the first tlist
6942			 * item found in the EC.
6943			 */
6944			foreach(j, plan->targetlist)
6945			{
6946				tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(j);
6947				em = find_ec_member_matching_expr(ec, tle->expr, NULL);
6948				if (em)
6949				{
6950					/* found expr already in tlist */
6951					pk_datatype = em->em_datatype;
6952					break;
6953				}
6954				tle = NULL;
6955			}
6956		}
6957
6958		if (!tle)
6959			elog(ERROR, "could not find pathkey item to sort");
6960
6961		/*
6962		 * Look up the correct equality operator from the PathKey's slightly
6963		 * abstracted representation.
6964		 */
6965		eqop = get_opfamily_member_for_cmptype(pathkey->pk_opfamily,
6966											   pk_datatype,
6967											   pk_datatype,
6968											   COMPARE_EQ);
6969		if (!OidIsValid(eqop))	/* should not happen */
6970			elog(ERROR, "missing operator %d(%u,%u) in opfamily %u",
6971				 COMPARE_EQ, pk_datatype, pk_datatype,
6972				 pathkey->pk_opfamily);
6973
6974		uniqColIdx[keyno] = tle->resno;
6975		uniqOperators[keyno] = eqop;
6976		uniqCollations[keyno] = ec->ec_collation;
6977
6978		keyno++;
6979	}
6980
6981	node->numCols = numCols;
6982	node->uniqColIdx = uniqColIdx;
6983	node->uniqOperators = uniqOperators;
6984	node->uniqCollations = uniqCollations;
6985
6986	return node;
6987}
6988
6989static Gather *
6990make_gather(List *qptlist,
6991			List *qpqual,
6992			int nworkers,
6993			int rescan_param,
6994			bool single_copy,
6995			Plan *subplan)
6996{
6997	Gather	   *node = makeNode(Gather);
6998	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
6999
7000	plan->targetlist = qptlist;
7001	plan->qual = qpqual;
7002	plan->lefttree = subplan;
7003	plan->righttree = NULL;
7004	node->num_workers = nworkers;
7005	node->rescan_param = rescan_param;
7006	node->single_copy = single_copy;
7007	node->invisible = false;
7008	node->initParam = NULL;
7009
7010	return node;
7011}
7012
7013/*
7014 * groupList is a list of SortGroupClauses, identifying the targetlist
7015 * items that should be considered by the SetOp filter.  The input plans must
7016 * already be sorted accordingly, if we're doing SETOP_SORTED mode.
7017 */
7018static SetOp *
7019make_setop(SetOpCmd cmd, SetOpStrategy strategy,
7020		   List *tlist, Plan *lefttree, Plan *righttree,
7021		   List *groupList, long numGroups)
7022{
7023	SetOp	   *node = makeNode(SetOp);
7024	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
7025	int			numCols = list_length(groupList);
7026	int			keyno = 0;
7027	AttrNumber *cmpColIdx;
7028	Oid		   *cmpOperators;
7029	Oid		   *cmpCollations;
7030	bool	   *cmpNullsFirst;
7031	ListCell   *slitem;
7032
7033	plan->targetlist = tlist;
7034	plan->qual = NIL;
7035	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
7036	plan->righttree = righttree;
7037
7038	/*
7039	 * convert SortGroupClause list into arrays of attr indexes and comparison
7040	 * operators, as wanted by executor
7041	 */
7042	cmpColIdx = (AttrNumber *) palloc(sizeof(AttrNumber) * numCols);
7043	cmpOperators = (Oid *) palloc(sizeof(Oid) * numCols);
7044	cmpCollations = (Oid *) palloc(sizeof(Oid) * numCols);
7045	cmpNullsFirst = (bool *) palloc(sizeof(bool) * numCols);
7046
7047	foreach(slitem, groupList)
7048	{
7049		SortGroupClause *sortcl = (SortGroupClause *) lfirst(slitem);
7050		TargetEntry *tle = get_sortgroupclause_tle(sortcl, plan->targetlist);
7051
7052		cmpColIdx[keyno] = tle->resno;
7053		if (strategy == SETOP_HASHED)
7054			cmpOperators[keyno] = sortcl->eqop;
7055		else
7056			cmpOperators[keyno] = sortcl->sortop;
7057		Assert(OidIsValid(cmpOperators[keyno]));
7058		cmpCollations[keyno] = exprCollation((Node *) tle->expr);
7059		cmpNullsFirst[keyno] = sortcl->nulls_first;
7060		keyno++;
7061	}
7062
7063	node->cmd = cmd;
7064	node->strategy = strategy;
7065	node->numCols = numCols;
7066	node->cmpColIdx = cmpColIdx;
7067	node->cmpOperators = cmpOperators;
7068	node->cmpCollations = cmpCollations;
7069	node->cmpNullsFirst = cmpNullsFirst;
7070	node->numGroups = numGroups;
7071
7072	return node;
7073}
7074
7075/*
7076 * make_lockrows
7077 *	  Build a LockRows plan node
7078 */
7079static LockRows *
7080make_lockrows(Plan *lefttree, List *rowMarks, int epqParam)
7081{
7082	LockRows   *node = makeNode(LockRows);
7083	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
7084
7085	plan->targetlist = lefttree->targetlist;
7086	plan->qual = NIL;
7087	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
7088	plan->righttree = NULL;
7089
7090	node->rowMarks = rowMarks;
7091	node->epqParam = epqParam;
7092
7093	return node;
7094}
7095
7096/*
7097 * make_limit
7098 *	  Build a Limit plan node
7099 */
7100Limit *
7101make_limit(Plan *lefttree, Node *limitOffset, Node *limitCount,
7102		   LimitOption limitOption, int uniqNumCols, AttrNumber *uniqColIdx,
7103		   Oid *uniqOperators, Oid *uniqCollations)
7104{
7105	Limit	   *node = makeNode(Limit);
7106	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
7107
7108	plan->targetlist = lefttree->targetlist;
7109	plan->qual = NIL;
7110	plan->lefttree = lefttree;
7111	plan->righttree = NULL;
7112
7113	node->limitOffset = limitOffset;
7114	node->limitCount = limitCount;
7115	node->limitOption = limitOption;
7116	node->uniqNumCols = uniqNumCols;
7117	node->uniqColIdx = uniqColIdx;
7118	node->uniqOperators = uniqOperators;
7119	node->uniqCollations = uniqCollations;
7120
7121	return node;
7122}
7123
7124/*
7125 * make_result
7126 *	  Build a Result plan node
7127 */
7128static Result *
7129make_result(List *tlist,
7130			Node *resconstantqual,
7131			Plan *subplan)
7132{
7133	Result	   *node = makeNode(Result);
7134	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
7135
7136	plan->targetlist = tlist;
7137	plan->qual = NIL;
7138	plan->lefttree = subplan;
7139	plan->righttree = NULL;
7140	node->resconstantqual = resconstantqual;
7141
7142	return node;
7143}
7144
7145/*
7146 * make_project_set
7147 *	  Build a ProjectSet plan node
7148 */
7149static ProjectSet *
7150make_project_set(List *tlist,
7151				 Plan *subplan)
7152{
7153	ProjectSet *node = makeNode(ProjectSet);
7154	Plan	   *plan = &node->plan;
7155
7156	plan->targetlist = tlist;
7157	plan->qual = NIL;
7158	plan->lefttree = subplan;
7159	plan->righttree = NULL;
7160
7161	return node;
7162}
7163
7164/*
7165 * make_modifytable
7166 *	  Build a ModifyTable plan node
7167 */
7168static ModifyTable *
7169make_modifytable(PlannerInfo *root, Plan *subplan,
7170				 CmdType operation, bool canSetTag,
7171				 Index nominalRelation, Index rootRelation,
7172				 bool partColsUpdated,
7173				 List *resultRelations,
7174				 List *updateColnosLists,
7175				 List *withCheckOptionLists, List *returningLists,
7176				 List *rowMarks, OnConflictExpr *onconflict,
7177				 List *mergeActionLists, List *mergeJoinConditions,
7178				 int epqParam)
7179{
7180	ModifyTable *node = makeNode(ModifyTable);
7181	bool		returning_old_or_new = false;
7182	bool		returning_old_or_new_valid = false;
7183	bool		transition_tables = false;
7184	bool		transition_tables_valid = false;
7185	List	   *fdw_private_list;
7186	Bitmapset  *direct_modify_plans;
7187	ListCell   *lc;
7188	int			i;
7189
7190	Assert(operation == CMD_MERGE ||
7191		   (operation == CMD_UPDATE ?
7192			list_length(resultRelations) == list_length(updateColnosLists) :
7193			updateColnosLists == NIL));
7194	Assert(withCheckOptionLists == NIL ||
7195		   list_length(resultRelations) == list_length(withCheckOptionLists));
7196	Assert(returningLists == NIL ||
7197		   list_length(resultRelations) == list_length(returningLists));
7198
7199	node->plan.lefttree = subplan;
7200	node->plan.righttree = NULL;
7201	node->plan.qual = NIL;
7202	/* setrefs.c will fill in the targetlist, if needed */
7203	node->plan.targetlist = NIL;
7204
7205	node->operation = operation;
7206	node->canSetTag = canSetTag;
7207	node->nominalRelation = nominalRelation;
7208	node->rootRelation = rootRelation;
7209	node->partColsUpdated = partColsUpdated;
7210	node->resultRelations = resultRelations;
7211	if (!onconflict)
7212	{
7213		node->onConflictAction = ONCONFLICT_NONE;
7214		node->onConflictSet = NIL;
7215		node->onConflictCols = NIL;
7216		node->onConflictWhere = NULL;
7217		node->arbiterIndexes = NIL;
7218		node->exclRelRTI = 0;
7219		node->exclRelTlist = NIL;
7220	}
7221	else
7222	{
7223		node->onConflictAction = onconflict->action;
7224
7225		/*
7226		 * Here we convert the ON CONFLICT UPDATE tlist, if any, to the
7227		 * executor's convention of having consecutive resno's.  The actual
7228		 * target column numbers are saved in node->onConflictCols.  (This
7229		 * could be done earlier, but there seems no need to.)
7230		 */
7231		node->onConflictSet = onconflict->onConflictSet;
7232		node->onConflictCols =
7233			extract_update_targetlist_colnos(node->onConflictSet);
7234		node->onConflictWhere = onconflict->onConflictWhere;
7235
7236		/*
7237		 * If a set of unique index inference elements was provided (an
7238		 * INSERT...ON CONFLICT "inference specification"), then infer
7239		 * appropriate unique indexes (or throw an error if none are
7240		 * available).
7241		 */
7242		node->arbiterIndexes = infer_arbiter_indexes(root);
7243
7244		node->exclRelRTI = onconflict->exclRelIndex;
7245		node->exclRelTlist = onconflict->exclRelTlist;
7246	}
7247	node->updateColnosLists = updateColnosLists;
7248	node->withCheckOptionLists = withCheckOptionLists;
7249	node->returningOldAlias = root->parse->returningOldAlias;
7250	node->returningNewAlias = root->parse->returningNewAlias;
7251	node->returningLists = returningLists;
7252	node->rowMarks = rowMarks;
7253	node->mergeActionLists = mergeActionLists;
7254	node->mergeJoinConditions = mergeJoinConditions;
7255	node->epqParam = epqParam;
7256
7257	/*
7258	 * For each result relation that is a foreign table, allow the FDW to
7259	 * construct private plan data, and accumulate it all into a list.
7260	 */
7261	fdw_private_list = NIL;
7262	direct_modify_plans = NULL;
7263	i = 0;
7264	foreach(lc, resultRelations)
7265	{
7266		Index		rti = lfirst_int(lc);
7267		FdwRoutine *fdwroutine;
7268		List	   *fdw_private;
7269		bool		direct_modify;
7270
7271		/*
7272		 * If possible, we want to get the FdwRoutine from our RelOptInfo for
7273		 * the table.  But sometimes we don't have a RelOptInfo and must get
7274		 * it the hard way.  (In INSERT, the target relation is not scanned,
7275		 * so it's not a baserel; and there are also corner cases for
7276		 * updatable views where the target rel isn't a baserel.)
7277		 */
7278		if (rti < root->simple_rel_array_size &&
7279			root->simple_rel_array[rti] != NULL)
7280		{
7281			RelOptInfo *resultRel = root->simple_rel_array[rti];
7282
7283			fdwroutine = resultRel->fdwroutine;
7284		}
7285		else
7286		{
7287			RangeTblEntry *rte = planner_rt_fetch(rti, root);
7288
7289			if (rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION &&
7290				rte->relkind == RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE)
7291			{
7292				/* Check if the access to foreign tables is restricted */
7293				if (unlikely((restrict_nonsystem_relation_kind & RESTRICT_RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE) != 0))
7294				{
7295					/* there must not be built-in foreign tables */
7296					Assert(rte->relid >= FirstNormalObjectId);
7297					ereport(ERROR,
7298							(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
7299							 errmsg("access to non-system foreign table is restricted")));
7300				}
7301
7302				fdwroutine = GetFdwRoutineByRelId(rte->relid);
7303			}
7304			else
7305				fdwroutine = NULL;
7306		}
7307
7308		/*
7309		 * MERGE is not currently supported for foreign tables.  We already
7310		 * checked that when the table mentioned in the query is foreign; but
7311		 * we can still get here if a partitioned table has a foreign table as
7312		 * partition.  Disallow that now, to avoid an uglier error message
7313		 * later.
7314		 */
7315		if (operation == CMD_MERGE && fdwroutine != NULL)
7316		{
7317			RangeTblEntry *rte = planner_rt_fetch(rti, root);
7318
7319			ereport(ERROR,
7320					errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
7321					errmsg("cannot execute MERGE on relation \"%s\"",
7322						   get_rel_name(rte->relid)),
7323					errdetail_relkind_not_supported(rte->relkind));
7324		}
7325
7326		/*
7327		 * Try to modify the foreign table directly if (1) the FDW provides
7328		 * callback functions needed for that and (2) there are no local
7329		 * structures that need to be run for each modified row: row-level
7330		 * triggers on the foreign table, stored generated columns, WITH CHECK
7331		 * OPTIONs from parent views, Vars returning OLD/NEW in the RETURNING
7332		 * list, or transition tables on the named relation.
7333		 */
7334		direct_modify = false;
7335		if (fdwroutine != NULL &&
7336			fdwroutine->PlanDirectModify != NULL &&
7337			fdwroutine->BeginDirectModify != NULL &&
7338			fdwroutine->IterateDirectModify != NULL &&
7339			fdwroutine->EndDirectModify != NULL &&
7340			withCheckOptionLists == NIL &&
7341			!has_row_triggers(root, rti, operation) &&
7342			!has_stored_generated_columns(root, rti))
7343		{
7344			/*
7345			 * returning_old_or_new and transition_tables are the same for all
7346			 * result relations, respectively
7347			 */
7348			if (!returning_old_or_new_valid)
7349			{
7350				returning_old_or_new =
7351					contain_vars_returning_old_or_new((Node *)
7352													  root->parse->returningList);
7353				returning_old_or_new_valid = true;
7354			}
7355			if (!returning_old_or_new)
7356			{
7357				if (!transition_tables_valid)
7358				{
7359					transition_tables = has_transition_tables(root,
7360															  nominalRelation,
7361															  operation);
7362					transition_tables_valid = true;
7363				}
7364				if (!transition_tables)
7365					direct_modify = fdwroutine->PlanDirectModify(root, node,
7366																 rti, i);
7367			}
7368		}
7369		if (direct_modify)
7370			direct_modify_plans = bms_add_member(direct_modify_plans, i);
7371
7372		if (!direct_modify &&
7373			fdwroutine != NULL &&
7374			fdwroutine->PlanForeignModify != NULL)
7375			fdw_private = fdwroutine->PlanForeignModify(root, node, rti, i);
7376		else
7377			fdw_private = NIL;
7378		fdw_private_list = lappend(fdw_private_list, fdw_private);
7379		i++;
7380	}
7381	node->fdwPrivLists = fdw_private_list;
7382	node->fdwDirectModifyPlans = direct_modify_plans;
7383
7384	return node;
7385}
7386
7387/*
7388 * is_projection_capable_path
7389 *		Check whether a given Path node is able to do projection.
7390 */
7391bool
7392is_projection_capable_path(Path *path)
7393{
7394	/* Most plan types can project, so just list the ones that can't */
7395	switch (path->pathtype)
7396	{
7397		case T_Hash:
7398		case T_Material:
7399		case T_Memoize:
7400		case T_Sort:
7401		case T_IncrementalSort:
7402		case T_Unique:
7403		case T_SetOp:
7404		case T_LockRows:
7405		case T_Limit:
7406		case T_ModifyTable:
7407		case T_MergeAppend:
7408		case T_RecursiveUnion:
7409			return false;
7410		case T_CustomScan:
7411			if (castNode(CustomPath, path)->flags & CUSTOMPATH_SUPPORT_PROJECTION)
7412				return true;
7413			return false;
7414		case T_Append:
7415
7416			/*
7417			 * Append can't project, but if an AppendPath is being used to
7418			 * represent a dummy path, what will actually be generated is a
7419			 * Result which can project.
7420			 */
7421			return IS_DUMMY_APPEND(path);
7422		case T_ProjectSet:
7423
7424			/*
7425			 * Although ProjectSet certainly projects, say "no" because we
7426			 * don't want the planner to randomly replace its tlist with
7427			 * something else; the SRFs have to stay at top level.  This might
7428			 * get relaxed later.
7429			 */
7430			return false;
7431		default:
7432			break;
7433	}
7434	return true;
7435}
7436
7437/*
7438 * is_projection_capable_plan
7439 *		Check whether a given Plan node is able to do projection.
7440 */
7441bool
7442is_projection_capable_plan(Plan *plan)
7443{
7444	/* Most plan types can project, so just list the ones that can't */
7445	switch (nodeTag(plan))
7446	{
7447		case T_Hash:
7448		case T_Material:
7449		case T_Memoize:
7450		case T_Sort:
7451		case T_Unique:
7452		case T_SetOp:
7453		case T_LockRows:
7454		case T_Limit:
7455		case T_ModifyTable:
7456		case T_Append:
7457		case T_MergeAppend:
7458		case T_RecursiveUnion:
7459			return false;
7460		case T_CustomScan:
7461			if (((CustomScan *) plan)->flags & CUSTOMPATH_SUPPORT_PROJECTION)
7462				return true;
7463			return false;
7464		case T_ProjectSet:
7465
7466			/*
7467			 * Although ProjectSet certainly projects, say "no" because we
7468			 * don't want the planner to randomly replace its tlist with
7469			 * something else; the SRFs have to stay at top level.  This might
7470			 * get relaxed later.
7471			 */
7472			return false;
7473		default:
7474			break;
7475	}
7476	return true;
7477}
